South Asian Business Association

A premier business society exploring every facet of the field — built on rigorous education and an enduring community of South Asian Cornellians.

Recruitment → Our Story

Our Mission

What we are building, and why it matters.

The South Asian Business Association at Cornell University aims to provide students with business and leadership skills through a comprehensive education process. We empower our students to explore business initiatives via mentorship from campus leaders, exclusive speaker events hosted by notable South Asian alumni, and membership in a community of lifelong friends. SABA is one of three national chapters, alongside Harvard and Columbia.

About SABA

What makes us special

More than just a business club. A community living out South Asian values by helping each other succeed in any facet of life.

Community

Join our tight-knit family to build meaningful relationships and lifelong friendships.

From wineries overlooking Cayuga Lake to nights out in the city, the bonds formed in SABA last far beyond graduation.

Meet the People →
01 / 12
The Education Series

Experience

Complete a case-study based education series to gain real-world experience while refining interpersonal skills.

01

Weekly Cases

Tackle real business problems across finance, consulting, entrepreneurship, and beyond.

02

Weekly Presentations

Sharpen the way you think, structure, and communicate ideas under pressure.

03

Mentorship

Learn directly from accomplished South Asian Cornell students and alumni.

2022

Our Founding Story

The South Asian Business Association was founded in 2022 to address the need for a business organization with a comprehensive curriculum that explored all facets of business, including finance, entrepreneurship, alternative asset investing, and real estate. This mission, coupled with a desire to bring South Asian Cornellians together into a broader community, led to the founding of SABA. Many of the most accomplished South Asian Cornell upperclassmen and alumni act as mentors to incoming students every semester to carry on the values that this organization was built upon.
